steps.title: Complete the main clause
steps.content: The subject is compound: Dawson's energetic gestures and his habit of chasing hit balls. The sentence needs a finite main verb to state what those behaviors did to the traditionally solemn umpire role.

steps.title: Supply the finite verb
steps.content: Choice B turns the action into a participial phrase and leaves no main verb. Choice C creates a relative clause after bounds. Choice D uses an infinitive without completing the main clause. The remaining choice supplies helped transform. The answer is A.
